mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal spending growth exceeds CPI inflation growth, consumer purchasing power expands and corporate revenue forecasts rise, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (IC < 0.01) when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and business investment, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods when INDPRO grows >1% quarter-over-quarter, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal rising construction activity and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations following a housing starts increase, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate indicates robust labor demand without overheating, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in >60% of periods when CPIAUCSL remains above 240, or the signal shows no predictive edge (IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate below 0.5% indicates weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when GDPC1 growth falls below the threshold, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05).
